name: Aider Repo Map
description: tree-sitter repo map ranked by graph/PageRank to feed the most relevant symbols

developer-org.comment: The repo map is a built-in component of Aider, the terminal AI pair-programming tool created by Paul Gauthier and now maintained under the Aider-AI org.

implementation-language.comment: Aider is a Python tool (~80% Python); the repo map is built with the py-tree-sitter-languages Python module.

first-release.comment: The tree-sitter + graph-ranked repo map described here was announced Oct 2023, replacing Aider's earlier ctags-based map that shipped with Aider in mid-2023.

status.comment: Actively developed; latest tagged release v0.86.1 with ongoing main-branch development as of 2026-07-09.

license.comment: Aider (and thus the repo map) is Apache-2.0 licensed.

website.comment: Official repo map documentation page; main site is aider.chat.

repository.comment: The repo map lives inside the main Aider repository (aider/repomap.py); it has no separate repo.

github-stars.comment: ~47.2k stars for the Aider repository as a whole (captured 2026-07-09); the repo map is a bundled feature with no standalone star count.

form-factor.comment: The repo map is a retrieval engine welded into the Aider CLI agent, not shipped as a separate product; third-party re-implementations (e.g. RepoMapper) exist but are not this tool.

standalone-usable.comment: The repo map is auto-generated inside Aider and cannot be bolted onto another agent; independent ports exist but Aider's own repo map is not exposed as a reusable component.

bundled-host.comment: Ships inside the Aider terminal agent.

install-mechanism.comment: Installed as part of Aider via pip (python -m pip install aider-install / aider); no separate install for the repo map.

retrieval-technique.comment: A repo-map technique: tree-sitter (AST-structural) extracts symbol definitions/references, a dependency graph over files is built and ranked (PageRank-style) to select the most central symbols within a token budget.

query-interface.comment: Not queried by the caller; the map is generated automatically each turn and re-ranked based on which files are in the chat, rather than answering a query.

returns.comment: Outputs a compact map of files with their key symbols and critical definition lines (signatures, class/function definitions).

structural-awareness.comment: Uses tree-sitter to parse code and extract structural symbols (functions, classes, signatures).

chunking-strategy.comment: Rather than chunking for an index, it extracts per-symbol definitions (function/class signatures) and includes the most important ones; boundaries follow syntax symbols.

reranking.comment: A graph ranking algorithm (PageRank-style over the file dependency graph) reorders symbols so the most-referenced identifiers surface — the reranking example cited in the guide.

index-location.comment: tree-sitter tags are cached locally in the repo (.aider.tags.cache); nothing is stored in the cloud.

incremental-updates.comment: Parsed tags are cached per file and only re-parsed when a file changes, rather than re-parsing the whole repo each time.

real-time-watch.comment: The map is regenerated on demand at prompt time reflecting current file state; there is no background file-watcher daemon.

vector-store.comment: No embeddings are used, so there is no vector store.

embedding-model.comment: n-a — the repo map is a tree-sitter + graph-ranking technique, not embedding-based.

mcp-support.comment: The repo map is internal to Aider and is not exposed as an MCP server.

agent-compatibility.comment: Usable only within its host agent, Aider.

ide-integrations.comment: Aider is a terminal tool; the repo map surfaces only inside Aider's CLI prompt context.

language-agnostic.comment: Symbol extraction depends on a tree-sitter parser for the language; files without a supported parser get no structural map.

supported-languages.comment: The 2023 blog listed 17 languages; Aider's README now states 100+ programming languages via its tree-sitter language pack.

code-leaves-machine.comment: The map itself is built entirely locally, but its content (code symbols and signature lines) is inserted into the prompt sent to the configured LLM, which is a cloud provider by default — so code egresses unless a local model is used.

local-only-option.comment: Map construction (tree-sitter parsing, ranking) is fully offline; combined with a local LLM, Aider can run with no external calls.

self-hostable.comment: Open-source tool that runs entirely on the user's own machine.

telemetry.comment: Aider's analytics are opt-in (you must agree on first prompt) and never collect source code, chats, or keys.

max-repo-scale.comment: A token budget (default ~1k, --map-tokens) plus graph ranking lets it summarize sizable repos, but the whole-repo PageRank computation grows with repo size, so very large monorepos can be slow to map.

pricing-model.comment: The repo map is free and OSS; Aider itself is BYO-LLM-key, but the repo map component consumes no paid embedding/model API of its own.

maturity.comment: A polished, widely used core feature of a mature tool, still evolving with frequent releases.

active-maintenance.comment: Aider is one of the most actively developed OSS coding tools; latest tagged release v0.86.1 with continued main-branch development (checked 2026-07-09).

community-size.comment: ~47k GitHub stars and a large active user base put Aider among the more widely adopted OSS coding tools.
